canonical_definition: Across the provided sources, SUPERB is characterized as a benchmark designed to measure spoken language understanding. The instrument is specifically described as focusing on "coarse-grained semantic understanding tasks." One paper offers a critique, stating that the benchmark "overlook[s] the importance of various acoustic features." In addition to its use in evaluating speech understanding, SUPERB is also studied in relation to speech recognition.
papers: Dynamic-SUPERB Phase-2: A Collaboratively Expanding Benchmark for Measuring the Capabilities of Spoken Language Models with 180 Tasks
Joint Fine-tuning and Conversion of Pretrained Speech and Language Models towards Linear Complexity
VoxDialogue: Can Spoken Dialogue Systems Understand Information Beyond Words?

definitions.text: A benchmark designed for spoken language understanding, which primarily focuses on coarse-grained semantic understanding tasks.
